465	\section{The tautological quotient on the charts}
466	\label{sec:grquot_chart}
467	
468	\emph{Infrastructure: scalar endomorphisms.} Realising a matrix of regular
469	functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules has no off-the-shelf primitive
470	in Mathlib; it is assembled from two small project-local helpers that turn a global
471	function into a scalar endomorphism of the structure sheaf, after which the matrix
472	is reconstituted entry-by-entry over a finite biproduct.
473	
474	\begin{definition}\leanok
475	[Global section of the unit module]
476	  \label{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
477	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.globalUnitSection}
478	  \uses{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
479	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) a global section of
480	  the structure sheaf. Viewing \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as the unit module
481	  \(\mathbf{1} \in \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}), the
482	  section \(a\) determines a global section of \(\mathbf{1}\): on each open
483	  \(V \subseteq X\) take the restriction \(a|_V \in \Gamma(V, \mathcal{O}_X)\), the
484	  family \((a|_V)_V\) being compatible with the restriction maps because
485	  restriction is functorial. This is the section of the unit sheaf attached to a
486	  global function.
487	\end{definition}
488	
489	\begin{definition}\leanok
490	[Scalar endomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)]
491	  \label{def:gr_scalarEnd}
492	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd}
493	  \uses{def:gr_globalUnitSection, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
494	  For \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the \emph{scalar endomorphism}
495	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) : \mathcal{O}_X \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is the
496	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear endomorphism of the unit module given by multiplication
497	  by \(a\). It is obtained from the global section of \cref{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
498	  under the canonical identification of endomorphisms of the unit module
499	  \(\mathbf{1}\) with its global sections, \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong
500	  \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\); concretely, on each open \(V\) it acts as
501	  multiplication by \(a|_V\). These scalar endomorphisms are the matrix-entry
502	  building blocks of the chart quotient.
503	\end{definition}
504	
505	\begin{lemma}\leanok
506	[Scalar endomorphism of the unit is the identity]
507	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}
508	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_one}
509	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
510	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the unit function \(1 \in
511	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the identity of the unit module:
512	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
513	\end{lemma}
514	
515	\begin{proof}
516	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
517	  Multiplication by \(1\) is the identity on every section, so under the
518	  identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the section \(1\)
519	  corresponds to \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
520	\end{proof}
521	
522	\begin{lemma}\leanok
523	[Scalar endomorphism of the zero function is zero]
524	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
525	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_zero}
526	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
527	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the zero function \(0 \in
528	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the zero endomorphism:
529	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\).
530	\end{lemma}
531	
532	\begin{proof}
533	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
534	  Multiplication by \(0\) sends every section to \(0\), so the corresponding
535	  endomorphism of the unit module is the zero map.
536	\end{proof}
537	
538	\begin{lemma}[Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication]
539	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}
540	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_comp}
541	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
542	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the scalar endomorphisms compose by
543	  multiplication: \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) \circ \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) =
544	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a\,b)\).
545	\end{lemma}
546	\begin{proof}
547	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
548	  Under the identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the
549	  scalar endomorphisms act as multiplication by \(a\) and by \(b\); their composite
550	  is multiplication by \(a\,b\), because the section ring is commutative and
551	  restriction maps are ring homomorphisms.
552	\end{proof}
553	
554	\begin{lemma}[Scalar endomorphism is additive]
555	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
556	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_add}
557	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
558	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) one has \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a + b) =
559	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) + \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b)\).
560	\end{lemma}
561	\begin{proof}
562	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
563	  Multiplication by \(a + b\) equals multiplication by \(a\) plus multiplication by
564	  \(b\) on every section, since the restriction maps of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) are additive;
565	  under \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) this is the stated additivity.
566	\end{proof}
567	
568	\emph{Infrastructure: matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf.} To realise the
569	\(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transitions one must promote an invertible \(d \times d\)
570	matrix of global functions to an \emph{automorphism} of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf
571	\(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\), exactly as the chart quotient \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}
572	realises the universal matrix. The next blocks assemble this matrix-to-morphism
573	functor and record its two structural identities -- that it carries matrix
574	multiplication to composition and the identity matrix to the identity -- which are
575	the algebraic heart of the bundle cocycle.
576	
577	\begin{definition}[Matrix endomorphism of the free sheaf]
578	  \label{def:gr_matrixEnd}
579	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd}
580	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
581	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
582	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a matrix of
583	  global functions. The \emph{matrix endomorphism} \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the
584	  endomorphism of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) whose
585	  \((p,q)\)-component, under the presentation of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as the
586	  finite biproduct of \(d\) copies of the unit module \(\mathbf{1}\), is the scalar
587	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,q})\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is
588	  assembled from these components by the universal property of the rank-\(d\)
589	  biproduct, exactly as the chart quotient \(u^I\) is assembled
590	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). This is the substitute for the (non-existent)
591	  Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) endomorphism of a free sheaf'' primitive.
592	\end{definition}
593	
594	\begin{lemma}[Matrix endomorphism carries multiplication to composition]
595	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
596	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_comp}
597	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
598	  For \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) the matrix
599	  endomorphisms compose according to the matrix product, with the order reversed by
600	  the contravariance of the component indexing:
601	  \[
602	    \mathrm{matrixEnd}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
603	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N \cdot M).
604	  \]
605	\end{lemma}
606	\begin{proof}
607	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
608	  The composition of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again biproduct-assembled,
609	  with \((i,q)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(p\) of the composites of
610	  components, \(\sum_p \mathrm{component}_{i,p} \circ \mathrm{component}_{p,q}\) (the
611	  categorical matrix product over the biproduct). Each composite of scalar
612	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the product,
613	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\circ\mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(ab)\)
614	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}), and a finite sum of scalar endomorphisms is the
615	  scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence the \((i,q)\)-component
616	  of the composite is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_p M_{q,p} N_{p,i}\bigr) =
617	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((N\cdot M)_{q,i}\bigr)\), which is precisely the
618	  \((i,q)\)-component of \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N\cdot M)\). The reversal of the factor
619	  order is the contravariance of the column/component indexing.
620	\end{proof}
621	
622	\begin{lemma}[Matrix endomorphism of the identity is the identity]
623	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
624	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_one}
625	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
626	  The matrix endomorphism of the identity matrix is the identity morphism of
627	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\): \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(1_{d\times d}) =
628	  \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}}\).
629	\end{lemma}
630	\begin{proof}
631	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
632	  The diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\)
633	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the off-diagonal components are
634	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the morphism with this
635	  component matrix is the identity of the biproduct.
636	\end{proof}
637	
638	\begin{definition}[Free-sheaf automorphism of an invertible matrix]
639	  \label{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}
640	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso}
641	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
642	  Let \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) be mutually
643	  inverse, \(M N = 1\) and \(N M = 1\). The \emph{matrix automorphism}
644	  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is the isomorphism of free sheaves
645	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) with forward map
646	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) and inverse map \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)\); the two
647	  composites are identities by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp} (turning the products
648	  \(NM\) and \(MN\) into the relevant compositions) and \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}.
649	  Its forward map is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) by construction
650	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}).
651	\end{definition}
652	
653	\begin{lemma}[Forward map of the matrix automorphism]
654	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}
655	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_hom}
656	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
657	  The forward map of \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
658	\end{lemma}
659	\begin{proof}
660	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
661	  Immediate from the definition \cref{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}, whose forward field is
662	  literally \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
663	\end{proof}
664	
665	\begin{lemma}[Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively]
666	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul}
667	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_mul}
668	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
669	  Let \(A, A', B, B'\) be \(d \times d\) matrices of global functions on \(S\) with
670	  \(A A' = A' A = 1\), \(B B' = B' B = 1\), so that \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(A, A')\)
671	  and \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(B, B')\) are automorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\).
672	  Their forward maps compose to the forward map of the product automorphism, with the
673	  order reversed by the component contravariance:
674	  \[
675	    \mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(A, A')_{\mathrm{hom}}
676	      \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(B, B')_{\mathrm{hom}}
677	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(B \cdot A).
678	  \]
679	  Equivalently, composition of matrix automorphisms realises matrix multiplication of
680	  the underlying matrices. This is the linkage that turns the matrix-level cocycle
681	  identity into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module isomorphisms.
682	\end{lemma}
683	\begin{proof}
684	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
685	  By \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom} the two forward maps are
686	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(A)\) and \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(B)\); their composite is
687	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(A)\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(B) = \mathrm{matrixEnd}(B\cdot A)\) by
688	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}.
689	\end{proof}

691	\begin{definition}
692	
693	\leanok
694	[Chart quotient \(u^I\)]
695	  \label{def:gr_chart_quotient}
696	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap}
697	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd}
698	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
699	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
700	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L898-L910).
701	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
702	  % "We next define a rank $d$ locally free sheaf $\U$ on $\grass(r,d)$
703	  %  together with a surjective homomorphism $\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$.
704	  %  On each $U^I$ we define a surjective homomorphism
705	  %  $u^I :\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I} \to  \oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
706	  %  by the matrix $X^I$."
707	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
708	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). On the affine chart
709	  \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) define the
710	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}\)-linear homomorphism of trivial bundles
711	  \[
712	    u^I \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}
713	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}
714	  \]
715	  given by left multiplication by the universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\)
716	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}): on global sections it is the \(R^I\)-linear
717	  map \((R^I)^{r} \to (R^I)^{d}\), \(v \mapsto X^I v\). Since the \(I\)-th minor
718	  \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\) is the identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\), the composite of
719	  \(u^I\) with the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates
720	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) is the
721	  identity; hence \(u^I\) is a split surjection of locally free sheaves, with
722	  target the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\).
723	
724	  \emph{Realisation.} The free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) and
725	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) are the finite biproducts (coproducts) of copies of
726	  the unit module \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_{U^I}\), indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\)
727	  and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Each entry \(X^I_{p,q} \in R^I\) of the universal matrix
728	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) is a global function on \(U^I = \Spec R^I\),
729	  obtained from \(R^I\) through the canonical identification
730	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R^I, \mathcal{O}) \cong R^I\); the corresponding scalar
731	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,q}) : \mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\)
732	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the \((p,q)\)-component of \(u^I\). Assembling these
733	  components over the index biproducts -- the universal property of the biproduct
734	  packaging a matrix of component morphisms into a single morphism of the free
735	  sheaves -- yields \(u^I\). This entry-by-entry assembly via the finite biproduct
736	  is the substitute for a (non-existent) Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) morphism of
737	  free sheaves'' primitive.
738	\end{definition}

theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
      = eqToIso (congrArg
          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj

The matrix-to-identity reduction (`hM`) is performed in a *small* context — over the single
chart `affineChart d r I`, with no pullback-of-free types in scope — so that the expensive

theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :

      = eqToIso (congrArg
          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
  -- The matrix part is the identity: `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` injects to the identity matrix, and
  -- `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙`. Proven here in the lightweight single-chart context.
  have hM : matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom
        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv).mapMatrix
        (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))

    rw [universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
  apply Iso.ext
  simp only [bundleTransition, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, eqToIso.hom]
  rw [hM, Category.id_comp, Iso.comp_inv_eq]
  exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom hφ _).symm

## Summary

The GR-quot resource-fix lane is **resolved**.

**`bundleTransition_self`** — the cold-build OOM/exit137 blocker — is re-proven with a genuinely leaner term that builds at **default heartbeats**, and the `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000` override is dropped.

**Diagnosis (the LSP was hiding it):** the proof *elaborates* fine at default heartbeats, but a real `lake build` exposed a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` — the old `.hom`-level cast chain produced a kernel-heavy term (≈227s, ≈13GB at the 1e6 override → OOM).

**Fix — iso-level proof:**
- New axiom-clean helper `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` proved by `subst` on **variable** morphisms, so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl`/`chartTransition`.
- Matrix automorphism collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the small overlap context (`hB`).
- `erw [hB]` bridges the Modules-diamond (plain `rw`/`simp` report "pattern not found"); the generic helper then closes `A ≪≫ C.symm = eqToIso ⋯`.

**Verified by a from-scratch `lake build`** (olean removed): `Built GrassmannianQuot (22s)`, *Build completed successfully (8319 jobs)*, peak ≈7.1 GB, both new declarations axiom-clean (`[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`).

**Investigative deliverable:** the whole-file build dropped **227s → 22s** from this one proof, so the OOM was **proof-local, not file-wide** — **no file split is needed**; the iter-061 C2 lane can run against the now-fast (22s/7GB) file.

**Sorry count: 4 → 4** (unchanged) — the four are exactly the do-not-touch C2 `bundleTransition_cocycle` (L664) and the three riders (L703/L713/L1207). No new sorries, zero axioms introduced.
